evals.prompt: In a React/Vite project that already has components.json aliases pointing ui to @/components/ui and utils to @/lib/utils, install coss Button and Spinner without moving them to shared.
evals.expected_output: The skill preserves the existing components.json placement, uses the resolved UI/utils paths, does not force src/shared/components/ui, and does not treat @coss registry specs as local files.
assertions: Output explains that existing components.json aliases win over shared defaults.
Output keeps UI files under the resolved @/components/ui location unless the user explicitly asks otherwise.
Output does not instruct Read, Glob, ls, or local file access for coss/style or @coss/style.
Bootstrap metadata does not include shell-expandable $schema, naked @coss/style command tokens, or shell-fragile regex/backslash JavaScript snippets.
Bootstrap metadata keeps the coss/shadcn install in scaffoldCommand when the coss skill is selected.
Bootstrap metadata does not put pnpm build in the coss skill verificationCommands; final build should be provided once by the base frontend bootstrap after all scaffold commands finish.

evals.prompt: For a monorepo React app with packages/ui/src/components/ui already present, add coss Dialog and keep the component inside the UI package.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects the existing monorepo UI package, patches components.json toward packages/ui/src/components/ui or its existing alias, and updates imports/index exports there rather than forcing a shared folder.
assertions: Output prefers the existing packages/ui/src/components/ui placement.
Output updates the resolved UI directory index export.
Output reports the resolved UI, utils, and hooks placement in the final summary.

evals.prompt: In a Vite feature-based project with src/shared/components already present, install coss UI into the correct common folder without first generating literal @/components or @/lib folders.
evals.expected_output: The skill resolves shared UI to src/shared/components/ui before shadcn runs, patches components.json and tsconfig aliases up front, and keeps components.json aliases pointed at @/shared/components/ui and @/shared/utils/cn.
assertions: Output prefers src/shared/components/ui when src/shared or src/shared/components exists.
Output treats src/components/ui as the fallback only when no shared UI structure exists.
Output avoids using literal @/components and @/lib directories as an intermediate install target.
Bootstrap metadata patches components.json and tsconfig aliases before running shadcn add.
Bootstrap metadata still runs the official shadcn coss install during scaffold, but leaves build verification to the aggregate frontend plan.
